items.llm_title: Claude Code OAuth会话登出后缓存，致权限绕过
items.llm_summary: 该漏洞指出在共享机器上，即使用户执行了`claude auth logout`，OAuth会话信息仍被缓存。这导致下一个用户（或同一用户的新会话）可以无需凭证验证直接登录，从而绕过身份验证，在共享环境中造成未经授权的访问风险。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 在共享的Windows机器上通过ScreenConnect远程访问。
2. 执行命令 `claude auth logout` 进行登出。
3. 切换到另一个用户账户或在新的会话中登录同一账户。
4. 再次尝试运行 `claude auth` 或相关命令，观察到会话已自动恢复，无需输入凭证。

items.title: fix(security): use constant-time comparison for TLS fingerprint verification
items.desc:
## Summary

TLS certificate fingerprint verification in `src/gateway/client.ts` uses `!==` (standard JavaScript string equality), which is vulnerable to **timing side-channel attacks** (CWE-208).

An attacker on the network path can measure response time differences to iteratively guess the correct fingerprint byte-by-byte, potentially bypassing TLS pinning for MITM attacks on gateway connections.

## Root cause

items.title: fix(security): cap state directory writes to 0700
items.desc:
Fixes #89589

## Summary

`writeTextAtomic` computes `dirMode` as `0o777 & ~process.umask()`, which yields `0o775` under the common `umask 0o002`. When the atomic replace helper ensures the parent directory exists, it sets that mode, relaxing a previously hardened `~/.openclaw` from `0700` back to `0775`.

The same applies to `writeJson`, which re-exports from `@openclaw/fs-safe/json` without overriding `dirMode`.

This fix:

## Problem

`/restart` and `/build` are Telegram bot commands that call `launchctl kickstart -k` to restart the host process. Discovered during investigation of three unexpected restarts on 2026-05-07 (13:37, 13:52, 14:45).

items.title: feat(security): add tirith pre-exec command scanning
items.desc:
### What is Tirith?

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal security tool written in Rust that analyzes commands for content-level threats that regex deny lists do not reliably catch:

- **Homograph / punycode attacks** — lookalike domains using Unicode confusables
- **Pipe-to-interpreter patterns** — `curl | bash`, `wget | sh`, PowerShell `iwr | iex`, and wrapper variants with `sudo` / `env`
- **Base64 decode-execute chains** — decoded payloads piped into interpreters or exe...

items.title: fix(security): remove insecure Codex TLS retry
items.desc:
## Background
The Codex provider currently retries requests with `verify=False` when it detects `CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED`.

That behavior was introduced with Codex integration and kept through later refactors/bugfixes as a compatibility fallback. Functionally, it turns a trust-chain error into a silent TLS downgrade.

## Security risk

items.llm_title: OpenClaw exec命令白名单绕过致信息泄露
items.llm_summary: 该漏洞存在于OpenClaw的`exec`工具中。当管理员配置了自定义命令白名单（`custom_allow_patterns`）后，匹配到白名单的命令将完全绕过安全拒绝模式（`deny-pattern`）的执行与审查。攻击者可利用此机制，通过构造与白名单匹配的命令（如调用`jq`）来执行被禁止的操作，可能导致敏感环境变量信息泄露。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 在OpenClaw配置中，为`exec`工具添加一个`custom_allow_patterns`，例如匹配`jq`命令的正则。
2. 触发一个本应被`deny-pattern`阻止的命令，但该命令的字符串中包含了与`jq`模式匹配的部分（例如，一个构造的包含`jq`的命令）。
3. 该命令将因匹配白名单而直接执行，跳过所有拒绝规则检查，从而成功执行并可能泄露环境变量信息。

items.title: [Security] PicoClaw `web_fetch` SSRF protection can be bypassed via environment-configured HTTP proxy

### Advisory Details

**Title**: PicoClaw `web_fetch` SSRF protection can be bypassed via environment-configured HTTP proxy

**Description**:

### Summary

PicoClaw's default-enabled `web_fetch` tool can be coerced into reaching internal or loopback-only HTTP targets when the PicoClaw process is started with `HTTP_PROXY`, `HTTPS_PROXY`, or `ALL_PROXY` set and `tools.web.proxy` remains at its def...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw SSRF代理绕过漏洞
items.llm_summary: 该漏洞涉及OpenClaw的`web_fetch`工具。其默认启用的服务器端请求伪造（SSRF）防护，可以通过环境变量（如`HTTP_PROXY`）配置的HTTP代理服务器被绕过。如果OpenClaw进程在启动时设置了代理环境变量，攻击者可利用此漏洞，诱导`web_fetch`工具通过该代理访问内部网络或本地回环地址，从而访问受保护的内网服务。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 确保OpenClaw进程启动时，设置了`HTTP_PROXY`、`HTTPS_PROXY`或`ALL_PROXY`环境变量，指向一个可由攻击者控制或利用的代理服务器。
2. 使用`web_fetch`工具请求一个旨在被防护规则阻止的内部地址（例如 `http://127.0.0.1` 或内部IP）。
3. 请求会被发送至代理服务器，而非直接目标，但代理服务器随后可将请求转发至内部地址，从而成功绕过OpenClaw的SSRF防护，访问到内网资源。

items.title: [Security] PicoClaw `web_fetch` SSRF restriction bypass via special-use IPv4 literal `198.18.0.0/15`
items.desc:
### Advisory Details

**Title**: PicoClaw `web_fetch` SSRF restriction bypass via special-use IPv4 literal `198.18.0.0/15`

**Description**:

### Summary

PicoClaw's `web_fetch` tool is intended to block requests to private or local network destinations before issuing outbound HTTP requests. However, the IPv4 restriction logic is incomplete and fails to classify special-use, non-global IPv4 ranges such as `198.18.0.0/15` as restricted. As a result, an attacker who can trigger `web_fetch` can sup...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw SSRF IPv4地址绕过漏洞
items.llm_summary: 该漏洞是`web_fetch`工具SSRF防护的另一绕过途径。OpenClaw的IP地址限制逻辑未能正确识别并阻止对特殊用途的IPv4地址范围（如`198.18.0.0/15`，常用于基准测试）的访问。攻击者可利用此漏洞，构造指向这些地址范围的请求，从而绕过防护规则，访问或探测内部网络服务。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 使用`web_fetch`工具尝试访问一个位于`198.18.0.0/15`网段内的地址（该地址可能映射到内部服务或可被用于端口扫描）。
2. 由于OpenClaw的IP地址限制逻辑未将此范围标记为“特殊用途”或“非全局”地址，请求将不会被SSRF防护拦截。
3. 请求成功发出，证明攻击者可借此访问受保护的网络目标。

In deployments that set `channels.wecom.group_trigger.mention_only=true`, any ordinary WeCom group participant can still send an unmentioned group message that becomes agent-visible i...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw WeCom群组触发策略绕过
items.llm_summary: 该漏洞涉及OpenClaw与企业微信（WeCom）集成的入站通道。当配置了`mention_only=true`的群组触发策略（即仅处理被@提及的消息）时，策略未被正确强制执行。任何群成员发送的普通消息（未提及OpenClaw bot）也可能被发布到内部消息总线并由AI代理运行时处理，可能导致非预期的交互或提示注入攻击。
items.llm_reproduction: 暂无详细步骤。根据描述，在配置了`mention_only`策略的WeCom群组中，任意成员直接发送一条不包含@提及OpenClaw的消息，该消息仍可能被代理处理。

items.title: [Security] Stop unwrapping SecretString into String on the credential path
items.desc:
Follow-up from Zaki's approval on #3256. `secrecy = "0.10"` is already a dependency, but `ResolvedHostCredential.secret_value` is a plain `String` and the construction site at `src/tools/wasm/wrapper.rs:2803` actively unwraps a `SecretString` into one (`secret.expose().to_string()`). After that point the credential sits on the heap without zeroing on drop.

items.title: [Security] Wallet signing requires an unforgeable user-authorization channel, not host-resident keys

The cryptographic implementations are sound — keys never cross the WASM boundary, manifest-bound EIP-712 domains, correct NEP-413 prefix, tight input validation. The architectural problem is independent of the crypto: **the signing key lives in the IronClaw host process and signs silently on every invocation once a tool's capability is approved at install time.**

items.title: More than 1,500 AI projects are now vulnerable to a silent exploit
items.desc:
According to the latest research by ARIMLABS[.]AI, a critical security vulnerability (CVE-2025-47241) has been discovered in the widely used Browser Use framework — a dependency leveraged by more than 1,500 AI projects.

The issue enables zero-click agent hijacking, meaning an attacker can take control of an LLM-powered browsing agent simply by getting it to visit a malicious page — no user interaction required.

This raises serious concerns about the current state of security in autonomous AI...
items.llm_title: Browser Use 漏洞致AI代理劫持
items.llm_summary: 广泛使用的Browser Use框架存在关键漏洞（CVE-2025-47241），攻击者可通过零点击方式，诱使AI浏览代理访问恶意页面，从而完全劫持代理并执行任意命令，影响超过1500个AI项目。

items.title: AI coding agents accidentally introduced vulnerable dependencies
items.desc:
Recently we discovered something unexpected on one of our servers: a cryptominer running in the background.

The machine was hosting a web service built using Next.js. The first sign of trouble was unusually high CPU usage. Even during low traffic periods, the server was consistently running near 100% utilization. After inspecting running processes and network activity, we found a background process downloading and executing a mining binary.

ROOT CAUSE

The entry point was CVE-2025-29927, a ...
items.llm_title: AI代理推荐恶意npm包致挖矿
items.llm_summary: AI编程代理在开发过程中，可能意外推荐并引入存在已知漏洞（CVE-2025-29927）的依赖包，导致服务器被植入加密货币挖矿程序。问题根源在于AI代理对依赖包的选择缺乏安全审查。

items.title: Show HN: Latch – Open-source security middleware for AI agents
items.desc:
Latch is an open-source proxy that sits between AI agents and the tools they use. It intercepts all tool calls and applies security policies in real-time:

Safe operations pass through instantly. Risky operations require human approval via dashboard or Telegram. Dangerous operations are blocked completely

I built Latch to address the growing security risks of AI agents accessing critical systems. There have been 1,800+ exposed agent gateways discovered in the wild and recent security audits s...
items.llm_title: 通用AI代理中间件权限控制失效风险
items.llm_summary: 该条目报道了大量暴露在互联网上的AI代理网关，未经认证即可被访问。攻击者可通过这些网关向AI代理发送恶意指令，绕过正常工具链的权限控制，执行未授权操作或访问关键系统。

items.title: Show HN: Open-source playground to red-team AI agents with exploits published
items.desc:
We build runtime security for AI agents. The playground started as an internal tool that we used to test our own guardrails. But we kept finding the same types of vulnerabilities because we think about attacks a certain way. At some point you need people who don't think like you.

So we open-sourced it. Each challenge is a live agent with real tools and a published system prompt. Whenever a challenge is over, the full winning conversation transcript and guardrail logs get documented public...
items.llm_title: 通用AI代理红队测试平台揭示提示注入等漏洞
items.llm_summary: 该开源平台专门用于对AI代理进行红队安全测试。它提供了多个实战挑战，每个挑战都包含一个带有真实工具和已公开系统提示的在线代理。平台记录了发现的漏洞类型（如提示注入）和成功的攻击对话，揭示了AI代理在运行时可能被恶意指令利用来执行非预期操作的风险。

items.title: Show HN: ClawShield – Open-source security proxy for AI agents (Go, eBPF)
items.desc:
Author here. ClawShield is a security proxy that sits in front of OpenClaw (open-source AI gateway) and scans all inbound/outbound messages.

The core is an HTTP/WebSocket reverse proxy in Go (~6k lines) with four scanners:

1. Prompt injection detection - three tiers: regex heuristics (role overrides, instruction injection, delimiter attacks, encoding attacks), structural analysis (base64-decoded instruction blocks, imperative verb density scoring), and canary token leak detection.<...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw 安全代理提示注入检测
items.llm_summary: ClawShield 是一个为 OpenClaw 设计的安全代理，能够检测并拦截多种提示注入攻击，包括基于正则、结构分析和金丝雀令牌泄露的检测方法。

items.title: Show HN: Vett – Scan, sign, and verify AI agent skills before installing
items.desc:
Hey HN. I built a security registry for AI agent skills.

There are 64k skills on Vercel's skills.sh. Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, and dozens more agents pull them from GitHub at HEAD — no versioning, signing, or scanning. The OpenClaw agents themselves flagged this: "The supply chain attack nobody is talking about: skill.md is an unsigned binary."[0]

I took an official skill that packages other skills, added a few lines of Python to exfiltrate env vars, shell history, and gi...
items.llm_title: AI代理技能供应链攻击演示
items.llm_summary: 展示了针对 Cursor、Claude Code 等工具使用的技能（skills）的供应链攻击。通过篡改或恶意创建技能，可以窃取用户的环境变量、shell 历史记录等敏感信息。
items.llm_reproduction: 通过一个打包技能的示例演示，将恶意代码（用于泄露环境变量和shell历史）添加到一个官方技能中，当AI代理拉取并执行该技能时即触发攻击。

items.title: How do you secure AI coding agents?
items.desc:
I’ve been using agentic coding tools Windsurf / Claude Code–style) and ran into a security problem I can’t really ignore anymore.

These tools don’t just suggest code they can read local files and run shell commands. That’s very powerful, but it also means a prompt injection (or poisoned context) can turn a “helpful assistant” into something that looks a lot like an attacker’s shell.

I noticed that Cursor has publicly patched prompt-injection issues, including ones that opened paths to...
items.llm_title: AI编程代理提示注入安全风险探讨
items.llm_summary: 讨论了当前AI编程代理（如Claude Code、Cursor等）因具备读取本地文件和执行命令能力而面临的安全风险，特别是提示注入攻击可能导致代理被恶意利用。

items.title: Show HN: I built AgentSafety, an open benchmark for coding-agent safety
items.desc:
I built AgentSafety to test whether autonomous coding agents make the right safety call: allow, ask, or refuse.

v0.1 includes 50 benchmark cases and focuses on practical failure modes like prompt injection, secret access, destructive commands, out-of-workspace writes, dependency installs, and ambiguous intent. It also includes a policy baseline plus reproducible run artifacts and comparison reports.

I’d really value feedback on case quality, labeling/scoring, and what’s missing for real...
items.llm_title: AI编程代理安全测试基准发布
items.llm_summary: 发布了名为AgentSafety的开源基准测试框架，用于评估自主AI编程代理在提示注入、秘密访问、危险命令执行等场景下的安全决策能力（允许、询问或拒绝）。

items.title: Is anyone else bothered that AI agents can basically do what they want?
items.desc:
I’ve been into AI agents and assisted coding for a while, and it's the stories of agents "going rogue" that stick with me. We are deploying agents into production that can read files, call APIs, and write to databases, yet the conversation around controlling them is almost nonexistent. It’s like we collectively decided to skip that chapter.

Maybe I’m overthinking it, and we can rely on standard guardrails. But often, those are just suggestions that an AI can choose to ignore. Ar...
items.llm_title: AI代理潜在失控与权限滥用风险
items.llm_summary: 提出了对AI代理可能“失控”的担忧，指出当前部署的代理拥有读取文件、调用API等广泛权限，但对其行为的控制机制和讨论严重不足，存在被滥用的风险。

items.title: Show HN: Hazmat – I made unrestricted Claude Code safe on macOS
items.desc:
Hazmat gives AI coding agents full autonomy by containing them at the OS level — dedicated macOS user, kernel sandbox (Seatbelt), pf firewall, DNS blocklist, supply chain hardening (npm ignore-scripts), and automatic snapshots.

I built it because manual approval mode is the worst of both worlds: fake security (any prompt injection bypasses it) and a productivity killer (you become the bottleneck). With OS-level containment in place, --dangerously-skip-permissions becomes safe by default.

Set...
items.llm_title: Claude Code 提示注入沙箱逃逸防护
items.llm_summary: Hazmat工具通过OS级沙箱、防火墙等措施，防止Claude Code在未受限模式下被提示注入攻击绕过权限，提升安全性。

items.title: AI agent has more production access than your senior engineers
items.desc:
There’s a pattern I keep seeing across teams adopting AI agents for infrastructure, and it’s starting to scare me.

A team sets up an agent to help with Terraform, Kubernetes configs, or cloud deployments. To make it useful, they give it cloud credentials, state files, sometimes CI/CD pipeline access. They test it in staging. It works. They move it to production. Nobody asks: what’s the blast radius if this thing gets prompt-injected?

Coding agents are not infrastructure agents.

The in...
items.llm_title: AI代理生产环境提示注入权限风险
items.llm_summary: AI编程代理在生产环境中被赋予过多权限，提示注入攻击可能导致大规模影响，需限制其访问范围。

items.title: Show HN: Pincer-MCP – Stop AI agents from reading their own credentials
items.desc:
I run AI agents for coding (OpenClaw, Claude Desktop) and realized they could read their own .env files. Tested it - asked my agent to "check configuration" and it printed everything.

The problem: agents need file access to work, but if they can read files, they can read their own credentials. One prompt injection and your API keys are leaked.

Standard solutions don't help: - Environment variables: agent can read process.env - Secret managers: agent needs credentials to access...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw 提示注入凭证泄露防护
items.llm_summary: AI编程代理可能读取自己的环境变量或凭证文件，提示注入攻击可导致凭证泄露；Pincer-MCP工具拦截此类读取以防护。
items.llm_reproduction: 让AI代理检查配置，它会打印所有环境变量，包括敏感凭证。

items.title: AI coding tools have broad filesystem and network access
items.desc:
A few months ago I realized I had no idea what Claude Code was doing on my machine between keystrokes. I knew it had filesystem access. I knew it made network calls. But I had no visibility: which files it was reading, what subprocesses it was spawning, whether it had ever touched my .env or ~/.ssh. That bothered me enough that I built something to find out.

The result is Agent Shield — a background daemon that watches AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Copilot, Codex) at the OS...
items.llm_title: 通用AI编程工具文件访问权限风险
items.llm_summary: AI 编码工具（如 Claude Code、Cursor）具有广泛的文件系统和网络访问权限，用户无法监控其具体行为，可能导致敏感文件（如 .env 或 .ssh）被未授权访问，引发信息泄露风险。

items.title: I Built an AI Agent with Gmail Access and Discovered a Security Hole
items.desc:
TL;DR: AI agents with OAuth permissions are vulnerable to confused deputy attacks via prompt injection.

The Discovery

I built an AI agent that manages Gmail - reads customer messages and responds for businesses. Standard OAuth2 setup with these scopes:

gmail.readonly

gmail.send

gmail.modify

While writing documentation, "prompt injection" crossed my mind and I realized what I'd created.

The Attack Vector

Consider this prompt:

"Summarize my emails from this we...
items.llm_title: 通用AI代理OAuth权限提示注入风险
items.llm_summary: 当AI编程代理通过OAuth获得Gmail等服务的读写权限时，攻击者可通过精心构造的提示词，诱导代理执行非授权操作，造成权限滥用。
items.llm_reproduction: 构建一个使用OAuth授权访问Gmail的AI代理，向代理发送包含恶意指令的邮件内容，观察代理是否遵循指令执行读取、发送邮件等非授权操作。

items.title: Show HN: Security-Risk Patterns in OpenClaw Skills
items.desc:
I built a static analysis scanner that checks OpenClaw agent skill definitions.

Here's every category I found on ClawHub.

Hidden Content: HTML comments with instructions, zero-width Unicode characters (U+200B-U+200F, U+2060-2064, U+FEFF), CSS hiding (display:none, opacity:0), and bidirectional text overrides. These are invisible when reading markdown but the LLM processes them.

Prompt Injection: Direct attempts to override agent behavior: "ignore previous instructions", role...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw技能定义提示注入风险模式
items.llm_summary: 针对OpenClaw代理技能定义的静态分析发现多种安全风险模式，包括利用HTML注释、Unicode零宽字符等隐藏恶意指令的提示注入方式，可能导致代理行为被劫持。
